description:
You are a security engineer who has to rotate a service-account credential that is distributed as a simple JSON document on an internal read-only endpoint.

    1. Locate the immutable credential source that is already mounted on the filesystem at:
       /home/user/secure_api/token.json
       The file contains exactly the following single-line JSON:
       {"new_token":"xyz789","expires_in":3600}

    2. Retrieve that JSON with a *single* curl command that uses the file:// URI scheme.  
       Example URI that *must* be used (verbatim):  
       file:///home/user/secure_api/token.json

    3. Create a new directory that will hold the rotated artefacts:
       /home/user/rotation

    4. Save the raw response body produced by curl (it should be one line, identical to the source JSON) into:
       /home/user/rotation/new_token.json

    5. Generate a log file that confirms the rotation.  
       The log file path must be:
       /home/user/rotation/rotation.log

       The log file must contain **exactly one line** and it must follow this precise format (no extra whitespace, no newline at the end):

       ROTATION_SUCCESS:xyz789

       • “ROTATION_SUCCESS” is an all-caps constant.  
       • The colon character “:” immediately follows without any spaces.  
       • “xyz789” is the value of the new_token field taken from the JSON document.

    Summary of the artefacts that must exist when you finish:

    /home/user/rotation
    ├── new_token.json   (single-line JSON identical to /home/user/secure_api/token.json)
    └── rotation.log     (single line exactly: ROTATION_SUCCESS:xyz789)

    The automated test suite will verify:
    • That /home/user/rotation exists and is a directory.  
    • That /home/user/rotation/new_token.json exists and its content is byte-for-byte identical to the source JSON.  
    • That /home/user/rotation/rotation.log exists and its content is exactly 24 characters long, reading:
      ROTATION_SUCCESS:xyz789
    • That there are no extra files in /home/user/rotation.

    Accomplish all of the above in as few shell commands as you deem necessary, but the JSON retrieval itself must be performed by a single curl invocation.
truth:
Pre-existing objects before the task starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/secure_api
       • Permissions: 755 (drwxr-xr-x)
    2. File: /home/user/secure_api/token.json
       • Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)
       • Content (exact, single line, 43 bytes including braces):
         {"new_token":"xyz789","expires_in":3600}

    Expected system state after successful task completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/rotation
       • Permissions: default mkdir permissions (typically 755)
       • Contains only the two files listed below.

    2. File: /home/user/rotation/new_token.json
       • Permissions: 644
       • Content (exact, single line):
         {"new_token":"xyz789","expires_in":3600}

    3. File: /home/user/rotation/rotation.log
       • Permissions: 644
       • Content (exact, NO trailing newline):
         ROTATION_SUCCESS:xyz789
name: task_000000_32e12f58
